title: [Feature]: Evaluate Azure Synapse and Microsoft Fabric sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Azure Synapse Analytics, Microsoft Fabric Eventstream, Fabric Lakehouse, and Fabric Mirror targets. nats-sinks does not yet have a Microsoft analytics-platform sink strategy.
proposal: Research whether Synapse and Microsoft Fabric should be separate sinks, shared analytics-lakehouse profiles, or built on Azure Data Lake/Event Hubs primitives. The design should cover staging files, SQL pools, lakehouse tables, eventstream ingestion, commit boundaries, idempotency keys, and Microsoft identity integration.
users: Microsoft analytics teams, cross-cloud data engineers, and operators who need auditable NATS event handoff into Synapse or Fabric.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires a documented durable acceptance boundary, such as committed table rows, accepted eventstream messages, or durable staged files plus ingestion confirmation.
security: Use managed identity where possible. Redact tenant IDs, workspace names, connection strings, SAS tokens, table names, and payloads. Document least privilege for storage, warehouse, and eventstream paths.
acceptance: Research separates Synapse, Fabric Eventstream, Fabric Lakehouse, and Fabric Mirror use cases.
The design chooses dedicated sinks or shared Azure analytics profiles.
Idempotency and duplicate handling are specified for each path.
Unit tests use fakes and no live Azure calls.
Documentation explains what is and is not durable enough for ACK gating.
tests: Research-only initially. Future implementation needs mocked SDK tests and gated live tests for the selected platform.
documentation: Add Microsoft analytics sink research under future sink docs and update roadmap.
closeout: Close after the research decision or implementation is released.
